text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
ROUTINE ONCOLOGIC SURVEILLANCE

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with known Stage IIIB left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant. Status post definitive concurrent chemoradiation (carboplatin/paclitaxel, 60 Gy in 30 fractions) completed approximately 19 months ago, followed by initiation of osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as consolidation/maintenance approximately 9 months ago. Prior CT approximately 3 months ago demonstrated a residual left upper lobe opacity measuring 1.6 x 1.2 cm with post-radiation fibrosis and interval decrease of right paratracheal lymphadenopathy. This study is for interval reassessment of known disease.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the uneventful administration of approximately 100 mL of nonionic intravenous contrast material (Omnipaque 350). Images were acquired in the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis, with a dedicated chest acquisition. Multiplanar reconstructions in coronal and sagittal planes were reviewed. Comparison is made to prior CT dated approximately 3 months prior and to the post-chemoradiation restaging CT.

---

FINDINGS

THORAX

Lungs and Airways:
The trachea is midline and patent. The central airways are widely patent to the segmental level bilaterally without endobronchial lesion.

Left upper lobe: There is a persistent area of heterogeneous opacity in the posterior segment of the left upper lobe in the region of the previously treated primary tumor. This now measures approximately 1.3 x 1.0 cm in axial dimension (previously 1.6 x 1.2 cm on the 3-month prior study; 1.9 x 1.4 cm on the initial post-chemoradiation restaging CT). The opacity has continued to diminish in size and attenuation since the prior study. Associated tethering of the adjacent pleura is noted, and there is a web of linear fibrotic stranding extending into the surrounding parenchyma consistent with evolving radiation-induced architectural distortion. The margins of this residual opacity are irregular and spiculated, though this morphology is stable and consistent with post-treatment fibrotic change rather than active tumor growth. No cavitation. No associated endobronchial component.

The previously identified left upper lobe satellite nodule is again not visualized and is considered resolved.

Remainder of the left lung demonstrates stable mild ground-glass opacity in the perihilar region, consistent with prior radiation field changes. No new consolidation, nodule, or pleural effusion in the left hemithorax.

Right lung: No pulmonary nodules. No consolidation. No pleural effusion. The right lung parenchyma is clear.

Pleura: Mild left apical pleural thickening is present, stable and consistent with prior radiation-related changes. No pneumothorax. No significant pleural effusion bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinum is not widened. The heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion.

Right paratracheal region (station 4R): Previously enlarged lymph node cluster now appearing as a confluent fibrotic-appearing soft tissue density measuring approximately 0.7 cm in short axis, unchanged from the prior study (previously measured 0.9 cm post-chemoradiation; 1.9 cm at initial staging). The attenuation characteristics are low and consistent with fibrotic/post-treatment change. No discrete rounded soft tissue nodularity to suggest active nodal disease.

Subcarinal region (station 7): Residual soft tissue fullness at the subcarinal space with a short-axis dimension of approximately 0.6 cm, stable to slightly reduced from prior. Attenuation consistent with post-treatment fibrous change.

Left hilar region: Mild residual soft tissue thickening in the left hilum, stable, consistent with post-radiation change. No new left hilar adenopathy.

No new mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy by size criteria.

Chest wall and osseous thorax: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No rib destruction. Chest wall musculature is intact.

ABDOMEN

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contour. The parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hepatic lesion. No hypodense or hypervascular mass. No biliary ductal dilation.

Gallbladder: Present, without gallstones or wall thickening.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances homogeneously. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal lesion.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in appearance. No adrenal nodule or mass identified bilaterally. No thickening.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size with homogeneous enhancement and symmetric cortical thickness. No solid renal mass. No hydronephrosis. No perirenal abnormality. Small bilateral renal cortical cysts, the largest measuring approximately 8 mm on the right, unchanged and consistent with benign simple cysts.

Retroperitoneum: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. No retroperitoneal mass.

Bowel: No bowel obstruction. No bowel wall thickening or mass. The appendix is normal.

Ascites: None.

PELVIS

Bladder: Normal wall thickness. No intraluminal filling defect.

Gynecologic structures: The uterus and ovaries appear within normal limits for age. No adnexal mass or pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Osseous pelvis: No suspicious osseous lesion.

---

IMPRESSION

1. Continued interval reduction in the residual left upper lobe opacity at the site of the treated primary tumor, now measuring approximately 1.3 x 1.0 cm, down from 1.6 x 1.2 cm on the study three months prior. The progressive diminishment in size and the stable irregular, fibrotic morphology of this opacity are most consistent with an ongoing treatment response to osimertinib in the setting of known EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma, with surrounding post-radiation architectural distortion. No imaging features to suggest active tumor growth or local recurrence at this time.

2. Right paratracheal (4R) and subcarinal nodal stations demonstrate stable low-attenuation soft tissue residua, essentially unchanged, most consistent with fibrotic post-treatment sequelae. No new or enlarging lymph node to suggest nodal recurrence.

3. No new pulmonary nodules identified in either lung field.

4. No hepatic, adrenal, retroperitoneal, or pelvic metastatic disease identified.

5. Stable mild left apical and perihilar post-radiation parenchymal and pleural changes.

Overall, the findings are in keeping with continued favorable response to maintenance osimertinib therapy with no imaging evidence of disease progression at this time.
